response.semanticDescription: The Button component is a fundamental UI element designed for user interaction, enabling a wide range of actions from submitting forms to navigating between pages. It supports several visual `variants` (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link) and `sizes` (default, sm, lg, icon) to fit diverse design needs. A key feature is the `loading` prop, which displays a visual indicator and disables the button during asynchronous operations, providing clear user feedback. Furthermore, the `asChild` prop, powered by `@radix-ui/react-slot`, offers exceptional flexibility by allowing the button to render as any child element (e.g., an `<a>` tag for navigation) while retaining all its styles and functionalities. This makes it a highly adaptable component for various interactive scenarios.

guidance.whenToUse: Use when an interactive element is needed to trigger an action, submit a form, or navigate to a new page. Ideal for primary, secondary, and destructive actions within an application. Leverage the `asChild` prop to style links or other elements as buttons.
guidance.accessibility: The button automatically handles `aria-busy` when in a `loading` state, indicating its non-interactive status to assistive technologies. When using the `asChild` prop, ensure the underlying child element (e.g., `<a>`, `<Link>`) provides its own appropriate accessibility attributes (e.g., `href`, `role`, `aria-label`) to maintain semantic correctness. Standard keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Space) and click interactions are fully supported.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Do not use for purely decorative elements or static text. If an element's primary purpose is just display rather than interaction, consider a simple link, paragraph, or div. Avoid overusing many different button variants on a single screen, which can confuse users.

minimal.description: A basic button with default styling.
minimal.title: Basic Button

response.variantDescriptions: {"variant": {"default": "Primary button for general actions and positive confirmations.", "destructive": "Red-themed button for dangerous or irreversible actions like deletion.", "outline": "Button with a visible border, suitable for secondary actions or less emphasized interactions.", "secondary": "Subtle button with a distinct background, for less prominent actions.", "ghost": "Transparent button with minimal visual impact, often used for tertiary actions or within toolbars.", "link": "Button styled as a text link, retaining button semantics but appearing as inline text."}, "size": {"default": "Standard size button for most common use cases.", "sm": "Small size button, useful for compact interfaces or less critical actions.", "lg": "Large size button, for primary calls to action or increased prominence.", "icon": "Square button designed to exclusively contain an icon, often used for toolbar actions or compact controls."}}
response.description: A versatile button component for triggering user actions, form submissions, and navigation, offering various visual styles and loading states.
